description_long: This object consists of two books: one is closed with a solid maroon cover, while the other is open, revealing aged, yellowed pages with detailed botanical illustrations. The open book's pages are slightly curled and appear to be made of thick, textured paper, with some pages partially turned or sticking out. The illustrations inside the open book depict plants and flowers in a classic, hand-drawn style, suggesting it may be a botanical reference or sketchbook. The closed book has a plain, unadorned cover with no visible illustration or text. Both books have visible spines and appear to be of medium thickness, typical of hardcover volumes. The overall appearance suggests these are well-used, possibly vintage books, suitable for use in a park, sidewalk, or urban study setting.
description: A pair of books, one closed with a maroon cover and one open displaying botanical illustrations on aged, curled pages.
description_view_0: The closed maroon book lies flat, while the open book displays several illustrated pages with botanical drawings and some curling or loose sheets.
description_view_1: The open book is viewed from the front, clearly showing botanical illustrations and the texture of the aged pages; the closed book is seen from the side.
description_view_2: The open book's pages are more visible, with one page partially turned outwards; the closed book is positioned further back.
description_view_3: The closed maroon book is prominent in the foreground, while the open book's illustrated pages are partially visible behind it.
attribute_spine_design: The spine of the closed book is maroon and plain, with no visible text or decorative elements.
